subject: axolotls
one_liner: Swipe-based matchmaking for a critically endangered salamander with a total addressable market of 812.
brand_voice_notes: Gill. is a consumer dating company that raised eleven million dollars to serve a species that cannot consent, cannot see well, and numbers in the hundreds. The copy never acknowledges the size of the market as a problem. It reports conservation genetics in the register of engagement metrics: inbreeding coefficients are compatibility scores, a studbook is a user database, a breeding program is a growth funnel. Never cute, never anthropomorphic beyond what the product requires. The axolotl's fixed smile is treated as a data-quality issue, not a charm. Every absurdity is delivered as an operating detail by someone who has already moved on to the next slide.

hero.h1: Meet every axolotl your genome hasn't already met.
hero.sub: Gill. ranks the remaining candidates by genetic distance, gill plumage, and limb count at time of photograph. Introductions are supervised. Chemistry is not something we can measure and we have stopped pretending otherwise.

features.title: Coefficient of inbreeding, surfaced live
features.blurb: Every profile shows how related the two of you already are. Under 0.0625 we call it a match. Over it we call it a match with a footnote, and surface it anyway, because the alternative is an empty feed.

features.title: Limb-count verification
features.blurb: Photographs are timestamped against a monthly limb census. A candidate who lists four limbs and arrives with three is not dishonest, only regenerating. Both numbers appear on the card.

features.title: Expression-neutral signal reading
features.blurb: The axolotl face does not move. It has never moved. We stopped inferring interest from it in 2024 and now infer interest from time spent near the filter intake, which correlates at 0.31.

pricing_tiers.name: Larval
pricing_tiers.tagline: for the axolotl still deciding whether to become anything

pricing_tiers.name: Neotenic
pricing_tiers.tagline: for the axolotl who is not going to metamorphose and has made peace with it
perks: Full coefficient of inbreeding, both directions
Priority placement near the filter intake

pricing_tiers.name: Founder Stock
pricing_tiers.tagline: for the axolotl who is the gene pool
perks: Genome licensed to three partner institutions
A conservation biologist attends the introduction in person
Quarterly board update on your offspring

testimonials.quote: I matched with a male from a different lake system. We have not spoken. The eggs are fine.
testimonials.name: Xóchitl
testimonials.title: Wild-type female, 4 years, Tank 11, Recovery Cohort B

testimonials.quote: Gill. told me my coefficient with the candidate was 0.31. I proceeded. Gill. logged that I proceeded.
testimonials.name: Bartholomew
testimonials.title: Leucistic male, on indefinite loan from a community college

testimonials.quote: I lost a forelimb in March and my introduction rate went up.
testimonials.name: Prudence
testimonials.title: Wild-type female, unranked, relocated from Lake Xochimilco

demo_screen_concept: A swipeable card stack set inside a tank-glass frame, lit from above like an aquarium at night. Each card is a full-bleed axolotl portrait with name, age, morph, and lake of origin, overlaid with three telemetry chips: a genetic-distance score out of 100, a gill plumage rating, and a limb count rendered as 3/4 with the missing limb annotated 'regenerating, est. 9 weeks'. A coefficient-of-inbreeding bar runs beneath the photo and turns pink past 0.0625 with the label 'proceed with documentation'. Three controls: pass, request introduction, and defer to the biologist. Requesting an introduction opens a modal that does not say it is a match, it says 'Introduction queued. Tank 11. A staff member will be present.' A counter in the corner reads 'candidates remaining' and decrements from 812.

images.rationale: Full budget of 4 used, all passed on attempt 1. Hero earned its slot because glassmorphism needs something behind the glass to refract; a flat CSS ground would have made every panel read as a gray box. Demo is photo-shaped (a swipeable candidate card stack), so three 1:1 axolotl portraits carry it, deliberately generated as three distinct morphs (wild-type, leucistic, melanoid) so the stack does not read as one animal repeated. The same three portraits double as the testimonial headshots, since the three named residents are the three candidates; no separate headshot slot was needed and none was generated.

items.prompt: Editorial underwater photograph of a single leucistic axolotl suspended motionless in deep still aquarium water, photographed through tank glass at night. Near-black teal water, hex 071A1E, fills the frame, with two soft caustic light blooms drifting down from above in pale aqua, hex 7FDCCB. The axolotl's feathery external gill filaments catch the light in soft axolotl pink, hex FF8FB1. One focal subject, centered slightly right, shallow depth of field, fine suspended silt catching the light, calm clinical stillness. Cool cinematic aquarium lighting, high clarity, no glare and no reflection of the photographer. No text, no letters, no numbers, no logos, no watermarks, no rendered UI screens.
items.verdict: pass: single subject held right of centre leaving clear space for hero copy, palette matches the tank-water ground almost exactly, caustic blooms usable as the live backdrop, gills land on the rationed pink. No text, no AI-render tells.

items.prompt: Editorial underwater portrait photograph of a single wild-type axolotl, dark mottled olive-brown skin with fine speckling, viewed head-on through tank glass. Deep near-black teal water, hex 071A1E, behind the subject, lit from above by a soft pale aqua caustic bloom, hex 7FDCCB. Dusky rose gill filaments fan out from the head. One focal subject filling the square frame, shallow depth of field, fine suspended silt, calm clinical aquarium lighting, high clarity. No text, no letters, no numbers, no logos, no watermarks, no rendered UI screens.
items.verdict: pass: head-on wild-type with correct mottling and full gill fan, tank-glass edge visible at left which reinforces the husbandry framing. Reused as the headshot for Xochitl.

items.prompt: Editorial underwater portrait photograph of a single leucistic axolotl, translucent pale pink-white skin and dark beady eyes, viewed in three-quarter profile through tank glass. Deep near-black teal water, hex 071A1E, behind the subject, lit from above by a soft pale aqua caustic bloom, hex 7FDCCB. Bright axolotl pink gill filaments, hex FF8FB1, fan out from the head. One focal subject filling the square frame, shallow depth of field, fine suspended silt, calm clinical aquarium lighting, high clarity. No text, no letters, no numbers, no logos, no watermarks, no rendered UI screens.
items.verdict: pass: leucistic morph reads clearly distinct from cand-1, three-quarter profile varies the stack, gill pink sits on the accent. Reused as the headshot for Bartholomew.

items.prompt: Editorial underwater portrait photograph of a single melanoid wild-type axolotl, deep charcoal-olive skin with a coppery sheen along the flank, resting on pale sand and viewed slightly from above through tank glass. Deep near-black teal water, hex 071A1E, surrounds the subject, lit from above by a soft pale aqua caustic bloom, hex 7FDCCB. Muted rose gill filaments fan out from the head. One focal subject filling the square frame, shallow depth of field, fine suspended silt, calm clinical aquarium lighting, high clarity. No text, no letters, no numbers, no logos, no watermarks, no rendered UI screens.
items.verdict: pass: darkest of the three, full-body resting pose gives the stack a third distinct composition, coppery flank keeps it from muddying against the ground. Reused as the headshot for Prudence.

design_direction.style: Glassmorphic aquarium husbandry console, night-tank mode. The page is staged as the client of a heavily funded consumer dating company that has decided a critically endangered salamander is an underserved vertical and prices a studbook by the seat. A deep tank-water ground (near-black teal) is lit from above by two slow caustic blooms, so the whole page reads as water viewed through glass rather than a webpage. Every panel is a true glass plate: backdrop-filter blur 18px, a translucent white fill at 6 to 10 percent, a 1px top-edge highlight at 18 percent that fades down the border, 18px radii, and a soft ambient drop shadow. No hard offset shadows, no borders heavier than 1px, no paper, no bevels, no molded plastic. One pale aqua carries the wordmark, headlines, hairlines, and every structural label, so the institutional voice reads as water-light. One rationed axolotl pink, the exact pink of external gill filaments, is reserved strictly for the single primary CTA per section, the live tank-status dot, the gill plumage indicator, and the inbreeding bar once it crosses 0.0625, and appears almost nowhere else as flat fill. Every figure that pretends to be husbandry telemetry (genetic distance, coefficient of inbreeding, limb counts, water temperature, candidates remaining, tier prices) is set in JetBrains Mono with tabular figures inside inset glass wells, so it reads like a tank readout, not marketing. Sora sets the wordmark and headlines in soft geometric sans with the confidence of a consumer app that has never been told no; Figtree carries plain, faintly clinical body copy. Motion is aquatic and slow: caustic blooms drift on a 22s loop, the tank-status dot pulses on a 3s cycle, glass plates rise 8px and fade in on scroll, the CTA lifts 1px and its glow widens on hover, the demo card stack settles with a spring on swipe, all gated behind prefers-reduced-motion. No light mode, no neon, no clay, no neumorphism, no bento lattice, no brutalist rules, no serifs, no marquee, no emoji. Reads like a company that files a species collapse under 'supply constraint' and lists a salamander's cousin as a compatibility risk.
design_direction.archetype: Consumer dating-app landing, Tinder-adjacent, staged as an aquarium husbandry console: slim wordmark nav with a live tank-status dot and a 'Request a tank introduction' CTA, split hero with a flat institutional claim left and a floating glass candidate card right (portrait, genetic-distance chip, gill plumage rating, limb count, inbreeding bar), features as three glass plates, testimonials as resident statements with headshots in glass ID plates, pricing as three husbandry tiers, a pink CTA band, and a handoff to the candidate-stack demo.
design_direction.motion_vocab: Slow caustic drift on a 22s loop behind the hero, 3s pulse on the tank-status dot, 8px rise-and-fade for glass plates on scroll, 1px lift with widening glow on CTA hover, spring settle on demo card swipe, all disabled under prefers-reduced-motion.
design_direction.hero_composition_notes: Two-column split above the fold. Left: a mono eyebrow well reading TANK 11 / POP. 812, an oversized Sora headline in pale aqua, a clinical subhead in Figtree, one pink primary CTA and a ghost-glass secondary link to the demo. Right: a single floating glass candidate card at a slight 3-degree tilt with a full-bleed axolotl portrait behind the glass, telemetry chips along the bottom edge, and an inbreeding bar. Behind everything, two caustic light blooms and a faint vertical glass seam at the viewport edges so the whole page sits inside a tank.
